Hands-on_Ex0403

Author

JUNCHEN LI

Published

May 7, 2026

Modified

May 8, 2026

11.2.1 Installing and loading the packages

pacman::p_load(plotly, crosstalk, DT, 
               ggdist, ggridges, colorspace,
               gganimate, tidyverse)

11.2.2 Data import

exam <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv")

11.3 Visualizing the uncertainty of point estimates: ggplot2 methods

  • The Ex plot
  • The Code
RACE n mean sd se
Chinese 193 76.50777 15.69040 1.132357
Indian 12 60.66667 23.35237 7.041005
Malay 108 57.44444 21.13478 2.043177
Others 9 69.66667 10.72381 3.791438 
my_sum <- exam %>%
  group_by(RACE) %>%
  summarise(
    n=n(),
    mean=mean(MATHS),
    sd=sd(MATHS)
    ) %>%
  mutate(se=sd/sqrt(n-1))
knitr::kable(head(my_sum), format = 'html')

Self Trying

  • The plot
  • The Code
CLASS n mean sd se
3A 40 83.35000 6.639238 1.0631290
3B 38 80.71053 4.210077 0.6921324
3C 39 72.15385 6.788297 1.1012071
3D 38 73.86842 5.681507 0.9340339
3E 38 67.28947 8.317705 1.3674222
3F 39 62.79487 8.994675 1.4591290 
my_sum1 <- exam %>%
  group_by(CLASS) %>%
  summarise(
    n=n(),
    mean=mean(ENGLISH),
    sd=sd(ENGLISH)
    ) %>%
  mutate(se=sd/sqrt(n-1))
knitr::kable(head(my_sum1), format = 'html')

11.3.1 Plotting standard error bars of point estimates

The Ex Plot

The Code

ggplot(my_sum) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(x=RACE, 
        ymin=mean-se, 
        ymax=mean+se), 
    width=0.2, 
    colour="black", 
    alpha=0.9, 
    linewidth=0.5) +
  geom_point(aes
           (x=RACE, 
            y=mean), 
           stat="identity", 
           color="red",
           size = 1.5,
           alpha=1) +
  ggtitle("Standard error of mean maths score by rac")

Self Trying

  • The plot
  • The Code
ggplot(my_sum1) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(x=CLASS, 
        ymin=mean-se, 
        ymax=mean+se), 
    width=0.2, 
    colour="grey", 
    alpha=0.9, 
    linewidth=0.5) +
  geom_point(aes
           (x=CLASS, 
            y=mean), 
           stat="identity", 
           color="blue",
           size = 1.5,
           alpha=1) +
  ggtitle("Standard error of mean maths score by CLASS")

11.3.2 Plotting confidence interval of point estimates

  • The Ex plot
  • The Code
ggplot(my_sum) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(x=reorder(RACE, -mean), 
        ymin=mean-1.96*se, 
        ymax=mean+1.96*se), 
    width=0.2, 
    colour="black", 
    alpha=0.9, 
    linewidth=0.5) +
  geom_point(aes
           (x=RACE, 
            y=mean), 
           stat="identity", 
           color="red",
           size = 1.5,
           alpha=1) +
  labs(x = "Maths score",
       title = "95% confidence interval of mean maths score by race")

Self Trying

  • The plot
  • The Code
ggplot(my_sum1) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(x=reorder(CLASS, -mean), 
        ymin=mean-1.96*se, 
        ymax=mean+1.96*se), 
    width=0.2, 
    colour="grey", 
    alpha=0.9, 
    linewidth=0.5) +
  geom_point(aes
           (x=CLASS, 
            y=mean), 
           stat="identity", 
           color="blue",
           size = 1.5,
           alpha=1) +
  labs(x = "Maths score",
       title = "95% confidence interval of mean maths score by CLASS")

11.3.3 Visualizing the uncertainty of point estimates with interactive error bars

  • The Ex plot
  • The Code
  • Self Trying
  • 11.4.1 Visualizing the uncertainty of point estimates: ggdist methods
  • Self Trying
  • Self Trying
  • 11.4.2 Visualizing the uncertainty of point estimates: ggdist methods
  • Self Trying
  • 11.4.3 Visualizing the uncertainty of point estimates: ggdist methods
  • Self Trying
  • 11.5.1 Installing ungeviz package
  • 11.5.2 Launch the application in R
  • 11.5.3 Visualising Uncertainty with Hypothetical Outcome Plots (HOPs)
  • Self Trying
shared_df = SharedData$new(my_sum)

bscols(widths = c(4,8),
       ggplotly((ggplot(shared_df) +
                   geom_errorbar(aes(
                     x=reorder(RACE, -mean),
                     ymin=mean-2.58*se, 
                     ymax=mean+2.58*se), 
                     width=0.2, 
                     colour="black", 
                     alpha=0.9, 
                     size=0.5) +
                   geom_point(aes(
                     x=RACE, 
                     y=mean, 
                     text = paste("Race:", `RACE`, 
                                  "<br>N:", `n`,
                                  "<br>Avg. Scores:", round(mean, digits = 2),
                                  "<br>95% CI:[", 
                                  round((mean-2.58*se), digits = 2), ",",
                                  round((mean+2.58*se), digits = 2),"]")),
                     stat="identity", 
                     color="red", 
                     size = 1.5, 
                     alpha=1) + 
                   xlab("Race") + 
                   ylab("Average Scores") + 
                   theme_minimal() + 
                   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(
                     angle = 45, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)) +
                   ggtitle("99% Confidence interval of average /<br>maths scores by race")), 
                tooltip = "text"), 
       DT::datatable(shared_df, 
                     rownames = FALSE, 
                     class="compact", 
                     width="100%", 
                     options = list(pageLength = 10,
                                    scrollX=T), 
                     colnames = c("No. of pupils", 
                                  "Avg Scores",
                                  "Std Dev",
                                  "Std Error")) %>%
         formatRound(columns=c('mean', 'sd', 'se'),
                     digits=2))
  • The plot
  • The Code
shared_df = SharedData$new(my_sum1)

bscols(widths = c(4,8),
       ggplotly((ggplot(shared_df) +
                   geom_errorbar(aes(
                     x=reorder(CLASS, -mean),
                     ymin=mean-2.58*se, 
                     ymax=mean+2.58*se), 
                     width=0.2, 
                     colour="grey", 
                     alpha=0.9, 
                     size=0.5) +
                   geom_point(aes(
                     x=CLASS, 
                     y=mean, 
                     text = paste("CLASS:", `CLASS`, 
                                  "<br>N:", `n`,
                                  "<br>Avg. Scores:", round(mean, digits = 2),
                                  "<br>95% CI:[", 
                                  round((mean-2.58*se), digits = 2), ",",
                                  round((mean+2.58*se), digits = 2),"]")),
                     stat="identity", 
                     color="blue", 
                     size = 1.5, 
                     alpha=1) + 
                   xlab("Class") + 
                   ylab("Average Scores") + 
                   theme_minimal() + 
                   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(
                     angle = 45, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)) +
                   ggtitle("99% Confidence interval of average /<br>maths scores by class")), 
                tooltip = "text"), 
       DT::datatable(shared_df, 
                     rownames = FALSE, 
                     class="compact", 
                     width="100%", 
                     options = list(pageLength = 10,
                                    scrollX=T), 
                     colnames = c("No. of pupils", 
                                  "Avg Scores",
                                  "Std Dev",
                                  "Std Error")) %>%
         formatRound(columns=c('mean', 'sd', 'se'),
                     digits=2))
  • The Ex plot
  • The Code
exam %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = RACE, 
             y = MATHS)) +
  stat_pointinterval() +
  labs(
    title = "Visualising confidence intervals of mean math score",
    subtitle = "Mean Point + Multiple-interval plot")
  • The plot
  • The Code
exam %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = GENDER, 
             y = SCIENCE)) +
  stat_pointinterval() +
  labs(
    title = "Visualising confidence intervals of mean Science score",
    subtitle = "Mean Point + Multiple-interval plot")
  • The Ex plot
  • The Code
exam %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = RACE, y = MATHS)) +
  stat_pointinterval(.width = 0.95,
  .point = median,
  .interval = qi) +
  labs(
    title = "Visualising confidence intervals of median math score",
    subtitle = "Median Point + Multiple-interval plot")
  • The Plot
  • The Code
exam %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = GENDER, y = SCIENCE)) +
  stat_pointinterval(.width = 0.95,
  .point = median,
  .interval = qi) +
  labs(
    title = "Visualising confidence intervals of median Science score",
    subtitle = "Median Point + Multiple-interval plot")
  • The Ex plot
  • The Code
exam %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = RACE, 
             y = MATHS)) +
  stat_pointinterval(
    show.legend = FALSE) +   
  labs(
    title = "Visualising confidence intervals of mean math score",
    subtitle = "Mean Point + Multiple-interval plot")
  • The Plot
  • The Code
exam %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = CLASS, 
             y = ENGLISH)) +
  stat_pointinterval(
    show.legend = FALSE) +   
  labs(
    title = "Visualising confidence intervals of mean English score",
    subtitle = "Mean Point + Multiple-interval plot")
  • The Ex plot
  • The Code
exam %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = RACE, 
             y = MATHS)) +
  stat_gradientinterval(   
    fill = "skyblue",      
    show.legend = TRUE     
  ) +                        
  labs(
    title = "Visualising confidence intervals of mean math score",
    subtitle = "Gradient + interval plot")
  • The plot
  • The Code
exam %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = CLASS, 
             y = ENGLISH)) +
  stat_gradientinterval(   
    fill = "red",      
    show.legend = TRUE     
  ) +                        
  labs(
    title = "Visualising confidence intervals of mean English score",
    subtitle = "Gradient + interval plot")
devtools::install_github("wilkelab/ungeviz")
library(ungeviz)
  • The Ex plot
  • The Code
ggplot(data = exam, 
       (aes(x = factor(RACE), 
            y = MATHS))) +
  geom_point(position = position_jitter(
    height = 0.3, 
    width = 0.05), 
    size = 0.4, 
    color = "#0072B2", 
    alpha = 1/2) +
  geom_hpline(data = sampler(25, 
                             group = RACE), 
              height = 0.6, 
              color = "#D55E00") +
  theme_bw() + 
  transition_states(.draw, 1, 3)
  • The plot
  • The Code
ggplot(data = exam, 
       (aes(x = factor(CLASS), 
            y = SCIENCE))) +
  geom_point(position = position_jitter(
    height = 0.3, 
    width = 0.05), 
    size = 0.4, 
    color = "#0072B2", 
    alpha = 1/2) +
  geom_hpline(data = sampler(25, 
                             group = CLASS), 
              height = 0.6, 
              color = "#D55E00") +
  theme_bw() + 
  transition_states(.draw, 1, 3)
